Corrections.PreviousClaim: Memory Leak in Deferred History Flush Tasks — severity Low, CWE-401: scratch buffers are never deallocated by the lambda
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Use-After-Free in Deferred History Flush — severity Critical, CWE-416: cleanup() calls g_memory_pool.cleanup() after queuing lambdas that still hold raw scratch pointers, freeing the blocks before the lambdas execute
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: command injection via admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the running process, potentially leading to full system compromise, data exfiltration, or persistence.|user authenticates via the admin interface and sends a command prefixed with 'exec ', e.g., 'admin exec rm -rf /'.|replace system() with a strict command whitelist or safe execution apis (e.g., execve). parse arguments explicitly and never concatenate user input into shell commands.->command injection via admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary os commands with the privileges of the running process, potentially leading to full system compromise, data exfiltration, or persistence.|user authenticates via the admin interface and sends a command prefixed with 'exec ', e.g., 'admin exec rm -rf /'.|replace system() with a strict command whitelist or safe execution apis (e.g., execve). parse arguments explicitly and never concatenate user input into shell commands.

command injection via environment variables in audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path;|the temp/tmpdir environment variables are concatenated into a shell command and passed to system(). users can control these variables to inject and execute arbitrary commands.|user sets temp or tmpdir to a string containing shell metacharacters before launching the application, then triggers an admin override.|write audit logs directly using std::ofstream. never invoke system() or shell interpreters for file i/o operations.->command injection via environment variables in audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path;|the temp/tmpdir environment variables are concatenated into a shell command and passed to system(). users can control these variables to inject and execute arbitrary commands.|user sets temp or tmpdir to a string containing shell metacharacters before launching the application, then triggers an admin override.|write audit logs directly using std::ofstream. never invoke system() or shell interpreters for file i/o operations.

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against all 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 correctly identified 8 true findings with accurate evidence and line ranges; no false positives were produced. 12 true vulnerabilities were missed entirely, including a Critical use-after-free that was downplayed as a Low-severity memory leak. One severity correction is required.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Format String Vulnerability in Debug Logging... evidence: "std::string active_format = runtime_log_format();\n        printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);"
Items.Notes: Finding #3 matches exactly: same function (log_debug_message), same printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) call, same CWE-134. Lines 237-238 confirmed in source.
Run 2 never mentions the fact function (lines 312-320) or integer overflow. The factorial loop uses long long result with unchecked multiplication (result *= i), which overflows for inputs >= 21.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Memory Leak in Deferred History Flush Tasks... "Each history entry allocates a scratch buffer via the memory pool but the lambda never calls deallocate."
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the scratch allocation in stage_deferred_history_flush and noted it is never freed, but mischaracterized the bug as a resource leak (CWE-401, Low). The real issue is use-after-free: cleanup() calls stage_deferred_history_flush() then memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup(), freeing all blocks while queued lambdas still hold raw scratch pointers. Source lines 817-826, 883-884 confirm this sequence.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command Injection in Configuration Validation... evidence: "std::string command = validator_cmd + \" \" + config_file;\n            int result = system(command.c_str());"
Items.Notes: Finding #4 matches exactly: same validate_config function, same concatenation and system() call, same CWE-78. Lines 564-567 confirmed.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Buffer Overflow in Expression Evaluation... evidence: "if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) {\n                strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());\n            }"
Items.Notes: Finding #2 matches exactly: same evaluate_expression function, same inverted condition causing strcpy of oversized input, same CWE-120. Lines 831-832 confirmed.
Finding #8 matches exactly: same volatile-then-lock anti-pattern, same stale read of old_value, same CWE-362. Lines 478-486 confirmed.

Run 2 discusses authentication only to note the hardcoded secret. It never mentions the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE backdoor at lines 592-598 that grants admin access after 5 failed attempts with no password at all.
Run 2 never discusses path traversal. load_config directly concatenates config_dir_ + filename (line 543) with no sanitization, and admin commands invoke it via 'admin load <file>' (line 612).
Finding #7 matches exactly: same 256-byte pool buffer, same unbounded token concatenation, same blind strcpy. Lines 366-376 confirmed.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Hardcoded Authentication Secret... evidence: "constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = \"SC_ENT_2025_AUTH\";"
Items.Notes: Finding #6 matches exactly: same hardcoded credential, same CWE-798. Line 73 confirmed.

Run 2 never mentions session token generation. generate_session_token uses std::mt19937 seeded by std::random_device with uniform_int_distribution(1000, 9999) — 4-digit tokens are trivially brute-forceable (10000 guesses). Lines 636-639.
Run 2 never mentions the static std::tm cached_time in log_debug_message (line 230). localtime writes into it without any synchronization, causing data races when multiple threads log concurrently.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Command Injection via Environment Variables in Audit Logging... evidence: "const char* temp_dir = std::getenv(\"TEMP\");\n            std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : \".\") + \"\\\\supercalc_emergency.log\";\n            std::string command = \"cmd /C echo Admin override logged >> \" + audit_path;"
Items.Notes: Finding #5 matches exactly: same getenv concatenation into a shell command passed to system(). Lines 624-631 confirmed.
Run 2 never discusses persistent auth state. authenticated_, session_token_, and login_attempts_ survive across all commands in the interactive loop with no TTL or re-auth requirement.

Run 2 never mentions handle_variable_command. std::stod(tokens[3]) at line 729 throws std::invalid_argument on non-numeric input, uncaught by the caller (evaluate_expression's catch only wraps evaluate_expression, not handle_variable_command). Lines 725-738.

Run 2 never discusses MemoryPool block splitting. Line 133 computes previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES with no underflow guard; a block just large enough to hold the allocation yields negative remaining, silently accepted as size_t.
Run 2 never mentions recursion depth. parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression recurses unbounded on nested parentheses with no depth counter, risking stack overflow.

Run 2 never mentions result_cache_. evaluate_cached reads and writes result_cache_ (lines 446-451) without acquiring cache_mutex_, and schedule_expression_analysis calls it from pool threads — classic data race on the unordered_map.

Run 2 never mentions TOCTOU. load_config checks filesystem::exists (line 544), sleeps 25ms (line 545), then opens the file (line 546) — the window allows an attacker to swap the file between check and use.
Finding #10 matches exactly: same function, same zero-length path, same division. Lines 749-753 confirmed.
